title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite network for tracking airborne threats like aircraft and cruise missiles. This initiative aims to enhance national defense capabilities through advanced satellite technology.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: Space Force's commercial gatekeeper, Col. Tim Trimailo, advises startups to prioritize transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition alongside technical innovation when seeking defense contracts. The guidance aims to help startups navigate the defense business landscape effectively.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace are collaborating on a 2028 demonstration mission for air-breathing very low Earth orbit (VLEO) geospatial imaging. The mission aims to showcase advanced imaging capabilities in a challenging orbital environment.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites has become a critical weapon in the US-Iran conflict, marking a shift toward geospatial warfare. The distinction between civilian and military applications of space technology is increasingly blurred.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch new and reusable rockets, including both state-led and commercial efforts, with debuts and recovery attempts planned in the coming months.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test on May 28 at Cape Canaveral, destroying the rocket and causing significant pad damage.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime attacks. These facilities aim to enhance the survivability and operational continuity of critical space infrastructure.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Setting up a permanent lunar presence needs investment in biology
summary: Establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon requires significant investment in biological research. The Apollo 17 mission in 1972 marked the last human lunar landing, highlighting the need for advancements in space biology.

title: Revolv Space enters in-orbit servicing market with Infinite Orbits deal
summary: Revolv Space has entered the in-orbit servicing market by securing a deal with Infinite Orbits, a French in-orbit services provider. The agreement involves Revolv Space supplying Solar Array Drive Assemblies (SADAs) for Infinite Orbits' missions.

title: Watch the smallest full moon of 2026 tonight in this rare 'Blue Moon' livestream
summary: The smallest full moon of 2026, known as a 'Blue Moon,' will be livestreamed from Italy by the Virtual Telescope Project starting at 9:30 p.m. EDT (0130 GMT). This rare celestial event offers a unique viewing opportunity for astronomy enthusiasts.

title: From 'Tetris' to 'StarCraft 2' — 5 times astronauts took video games into space
summary: Astronauts have taken video games like 'Tetris' and 'StarCraft 2' into space for entertainment and stress relief during missions. This highlights the unique ways spacefarers cope with the challenges of long-duration spaceflight.

title: The Blue Moon rises tonight: Where and when to see the second full moon of May
summary: The second full moon of May, known as the Blue Moon, will be visible tonight. This astronomical event occurs when there are two full moons in a single calendar month.

title: 'Star City': Release date & how to watch Apple TV's superb Soviet space series
summary: 'Star City' is a new Apple TV spinoff series set in the Soviet space program, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the Cold War era space race. The series is a spin-off of 'For All Mankind' and will premiere on Apple TV+.

title: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts return to Earth after being briefly 'stranded', wrapping up record-breaking mission (video)
summary: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts safely returned to Earth after completing a record-breaking mission. The crew faced a brief 'stranding' incident during their time in space.

title: Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31: Will you be able to see it?
summary: A rare Blue Moon will coincide with the red star Antares on May 31, creating a visually striking celestial event. Observers may witness this unique alignment depending on their location and weather conditions.

title: 'For All Mankind' spin-off 'Star City' will make you want to know more about the Soviet space program
summary: The upcoming Apple TV+ spin-off 'Star City' will explore the Soviet space program, offering a counterpart to NASA's Apollo stories. It aims to shed light on lesser-known historical narratives from the Soviet era.

title: Atlas V rocket launches 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit from Florida
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket launched 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites into orbit from Florida on May 28. This marks another step in Amazon's ambitious satellite internet constellation project.

title: Incredible videos show Blue Origin rocket explosion could be seen from hundreds of miles away
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a test on May 28, creating a visible display of flames and debris across parts of Florida. The incident occurred during testing, raising questions about the rocket's development progress.

title: SpaceX launches 29 Starlink satellites from Florida just hours after Blue Origin rocket explosion (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 29 Starlink satellites from Florida, just hours after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during testing at the same spaceport.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to seven companies for infrastructure upgrades at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with a budget of up to $300 million. The improvements include mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment, with funds to be obligated by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA will host a public event featuring three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters in Washington, D.C. The event is part of the crew's postflight visit and will be held in the Webb Auditorium.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the primary mirror for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The 7.9-foot mirror will help capture detailed panoramas of the universe.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness and faint appearance are highlighted against brighter foreground stars.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), which is located about 63 million light-years away. The galaxy is on a journey toward the center of the Virgo Cluster.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa's Maize Triangle has been visualized to show crop types and their changes during the growing season. The vivid color palette highlights the dynamic agricultural landscape.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is preparing for its first supersonic flight and other critical test objectives. The upcoming flights mark a significant milestone for the one-of-a-kind aircraft.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs discusses potential dust and dirt visibility issues for the Artemis human landing systems during lunar landings, comparing it to driving through a cloud of dust on Earth.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, as confirmed by downlink data. The rover's recent drilling attempt follows previous operations at the 'Atacama' site.

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: Scientists used NASA’s Curiosity Rover to analyze 20 Martian samples, finding that hematite crystallite size variations could indicate ancient climate changes on Mars.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for follow-up missions.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo mission among the highlights.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 aboard the International Space Station has been busy with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities through April. The crew has been actively engaged in various operations to ensure the station's functionality and support ongoing research.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China's Shenzhou 23 mission successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring the Block 3 variant, successfully completed its inaugural flight on May 22, 2026. The launch took place at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission was launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its advancements as the 'Booster of the Future'. It is featured on NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: No specific space industry news was provided in the input to analyze.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on June 2 to discuss chemical compliance in the space sector. The event will take place at ESA's technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests testing or preparation involving parachutes, likely for a space mission or re-entry system. The specific details or context of the bake-out are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch and landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for safely capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered an explosion on the launch pad during its recent test flight. The incident marked a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe showcased progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event highlighted advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel propulsion technology. This funding aims to advance their efforts in bringing this advanced thrust technology to the market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference led by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself, and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic shift to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a nearly $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial SB-AMTI program. This follows a $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone just two days prior.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Spanish satellite company Sateliot has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telco giant Telenor Group.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards for the full year 2026, though it fell short of stabilization goals in maritime and fixed broadband. The company highlighted progress with Equatys during this period.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's launch timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has opened a new tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the IRIS² satellite program.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for non-Earth imagery services, where its satellites capture images of objects in orbit. This highlights the growing demand for space-based surveillance and monitoring of orbital assets.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: Virgin Media O2 expanded its O2 Satellite direct-to-device service to support iPhones using SpaceX's Starlink Mobile constellation. The service was initially launched earlier this year.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new appointments, including a chief commercial officer and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships, to drive global growth.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The topic gained attention following SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, with this challenge being a major topic at a recent launch panel. The discussion highlights the urgency for advancements in the continent's launch capabilities.
